subject: Chemistry
title: The Elephant Toothpaste Reaction
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Elephant Toothpaste Reaction.

hook.text: Mix hydrogen peroxide with potassium iodide and dish soap. A giant foam snake erupts from the container.

buildup.text: Hydrogen peroxide slowly decomposes into water and oxygen. The iodide ion speeds this up dramatically.

discovery.text: Soap traps the released oxygen as bubbles, creating a massive foam eruption that looks like toothpaste.

twist.text: It's a catalytic decomposition. The iodide isn't consumed—it speeds the reaction without being used up.

climax.text: The foam is warm to the touch. The reaction is exothermic—it releases heat as peroxide breaks down.

punchline.text: Peroxide, iodide, soap. A foam volcano in seconds. Chemistry's best show.

quiz.question: What role does potassium iodide play in elephant toothpaste?
options: It acts as a catalyst, speeding up hydrogen peroxide decomposition
It changes the color of the reaction
